reasoning: Your symptoms — clear runny nose, sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, no fever, no colored discharge, worse outdoors — are classic signs of seasonal or environmental allergies (allergic rhinitis). Austin is indeed notorious for high pollen counts (cedar, oak, ragweed, grasses) year-round. Your childhood eczema history also makes you more prone to allergies (this is called the 'atopic triad'). Over-the-counter options that can help include: (1) a non-drowsy antihistamine like cetirizine (Zyrtec), loratadine (Claritin), or fexofenadine (Allegra) taken daily, (2) a nasal corticosteroid spray like fluticasone (Flonase) or triamcinolone (Nasacort) — these work best with consistent daily use for a week or more, and (3) antihistamine eye drops like ketotifen (Zaditor) for the itchy eyes. You can also minimize exposure by keeping windows closed, showering after being outdoors, and checking local pollen counts. If OTC treatments don't provide adequate relief after a few weeks, or symptoms significantly worsen, consider scheduling an appointment with an allergist (Category C at that point) for possible allergy testing and prescription options.
